stemHtml: Sea smoke can occasionally drift onto land and affect coastal aerodromes. It forms when...

options.a: relatively warm, moist air moves over water with a surface temperature that is below the dew point of the moving air.
options.b: saturated air from the surface of the sea is forced to rise as it meets coastal cliffs, forming fog that drifts inland.
options.c: sea spray from breaking waves in strong surface winds forms a mist that drifts across the surface.
options.d: evaporated vapour from a warm water surface condenses on contact with much colder air, and is unable to rise because of a surface inversion.
